description: Safe defaults for CORS configuration. Encode the allowlist in code or config, never derived from a database.

anti_patterns.rule: Access-Control-Allow-Origin=* combined with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials=true is forbidden
Origin reflected without allowlist check is equivalent to *
Origin=null grants access to sandboxed iframes, data URIs, file URLs
*.example.com regex requires subdomain takeover controls and explicit ownership
Access-Control-Expose-Headers leaks internal headers (X-Internal-User, X-Trace, etc.)
CORS is browser-side; authentication is the actual gate. Don't rely on CORS to refuse server-to-server callers
